regions.us-ports: Today in Miami I report southeast winds 5 kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, and that active thunderstorm signal also places Port Everglades, Port Canaveral, and Tampa Bay on my immediate watch. For today's embarkation I am using the Day 1 forecast of SSE 12 kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability in Miami to stage gangway checks and lightning watches at Port Everglades and Port Canaveral. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Day 2 shows SSE 14 kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability in Miami, so I will keep Port Everglades, Port Canaveral, and Tampa Bay on operational alert for gusty SSE winds and lightning that could require sequencing of tender operations. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 tightens to a south wind 18 kt with thunderstorms and 23% rain probability in Miami, and I will brief ship and terminal teams on S 18 kt winds and the 23% rain probability as we evaluate tendering and shore excursion timelines.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today SIGNIFICANT WEATHER TODAY: thunderstorms, less than 10% rain probability | SIGNIFICANT WEATHER DAY 2: thunderstorms, less than 10% rain probability, with Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Key West, Grand Cayman and Ocho Rios listed as the most affected ports while current Nassau observations show ESE 5kt, clear skies and less than 10% rain probability. Today, given Nassau ESE 5kt, clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, I am directing normal embarkation and disembarkation procedures in Nassau and Freeport and routine shore excursion loading at Bimini and the Berry Islands. Over the next 24 hours Nassau is forecast SE 13kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability and through 24–48 hours Nassau is forecast SE 11kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, so I will monitor wind-driven vessel motions for tender operations at Key West, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios and Puerto Plata. Beyond 48 hours Nassau outlook shows SE 14kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability, and I will keep ship and shoreside teams updated for La Romana, Santo Domingo and Samaná based on the SE 14kt and drizzle trend.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today I am issuing a PRIORITY ALERT: SIGNIFICANT WEATHER DAY 2: thunderstorms, 14% rain probability for San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix, and San Juan's current conditions are ESE 8kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability. I am keeping embarkation and gangway operations in San Juan active under the present ESE 8kt winds, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability while staging extra crew for tendering at St. Thomas and St. Croix as a precaution for the E 17kt winds forecast with the Day 2 thunderstorms. Over the next 24 to 48 hours San Juan shows Day 1 E 18kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability followed by Day 2 E 17kt with thunderstorms and 14% rain probability, so I am planning wind-driven operational adjustments for tender schedules at St. Maarten and Antigua to account for the E to E winds near 17-18kt. Beyond 48 hours San Juan Day 3 remains E 17kt with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability and I will update the bridge and shore teams if the E 17kt forecast or sky condition changes.
regions.western-caribbean: Today I am issuing a priority alert: Significant weather today, thunderstorms, 17% rain probability; Day 2, thunderstorms, 16% rain probability; Day 3, thunderstorms, 10% rain probability for Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City, and Cozumel's current conditions are SE 10kt and overcast. For operations today I note Cozumel's Day 1 forecast of SE 13kt winds, thunderstorms, and 17% rain probability with overcast skies, and I will hold final decisions on gangway and tender deployments in Cozumel and Costa Maya to standard safety checks given the SE 13kt wind vector. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Cozumel's Day 2 outlook shows SE 13kt winds, thunderstorms, and 16% rain probability, so I will monitor those SE 13kt conditions for Roatan and Belize City when scheduling shore excursion embark times and vessel maneuvering. Beyond 48 hours the trend eases to ESE 9kt with thunderstorms and 10% rain probability for Cozumel, and that ESE 9kt forecast will guide embarkation and disembarkation planning for Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City compared with the earlier SE 13kt forecasts.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today I am issuing a PRIORITY ALERT: Significant Weather Day 3 forecasts thunderstorms with E 19kt winds and a 13% rain probability on Day 3, with Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena the ports most affected. Today I am monitoring Aruba's current E 17kt east winds, partly cloudy skies, and a 14% rain probability and have directed gangway and tender teams to account for the E 17kt wind during embarkation and shore excursions at Aruba and Curacao. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Aruba is forecast to see E 18kt winds with drizzle and a 14% rain probability on Day 1 and E 18kt winds with drizzle and a 19% rain probability on Day 2, so I will prioritize docking and tender schedules for Curacao and Bonaire around the sustained E 18kt winds. Beyond 48 hours the trend toward E 19kt winds with thunderstorms and a 13% rain probability on Day 3 for Aruba means I will advise shipboard operations to prepare for thunderstorm timing and adjust outdoor excursion windows for Cartagena and Bonaire accordingly.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today I am issuing a priority alert: SIGNIFICANT WEATHER TODAY thunderstorms, 58% rain probability may affect Barbados, St. Lucia, and Martinique, with current conditions recorded as ENE 10kt and drizzle. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 1 forecasts W 11kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 forecasts W 12kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, and I will coordinate docking and tendering windows for Ensenada and Punta Banda while sequencing shore excursions to Islas Todos Santos. regions.mazatlan: Today I am issuing a priority alert for significant weather Day 3: rain showers, 30% rain probability that may affect Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, while current conditions at port are SE 3kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability. regions.puerto-vallarta: Today I am monitoring ENE 3kt winds with a partly cloudy sky and a 22% rain probability, and I am authorizing embarkation and most shore excursions in Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita under the light ENE flow and intermittent sun breaks. regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today I am seeing NNE 7kt winds, overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability at Piraeus (Athens), so I am keeping embarkation and gangway operations in Athens/Piraeus on a normal schedule under light north-northeast wind loading. regions.southeast-alaska: Today I am monitoring Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway with winds from the west at 2 kt, overcast skies in Juneau, and a 24% rain probability in Juneau, and that W 2 kt wind profile points to straightforward docking and tendering operations for these ports. Over the next 24 hours Juneau is forecast WNW 6 kt with drizzle and a 24% rain probability on Day 1, then WNW 8 kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability on Day 2, keeping winds well below Small Craft Advisory levels for Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway.
